title: Ship intent-conformance (the inversion) — prove code satisfies the recorded WHY over the graph
context: After the research roadmap was exhausted, an unbounded-thinking pass reframed Hunch's endgame: as code generation commoditizes, value moves from the code to the JUDGMENT (why this, what's forbidden, what failed). The deepest move that falls out is the INVERSION — treat recorded intent as authoritative and code as a projection that must conform to it. This is the first concrete, shippable experiment toward that: a new correctness class beside types and tests.
decision: Add intent-conformance. A Decision carries optional `conformance` predicates (assert: calls / not-calls / imports / not-imports / exists; subject/object as symbol name|id|file:name; transitive flag) that COMPILE the decision's intent into a deterministic reachability check over the symbol/dependency graph Hunch already builds. checkConformance(store) evaluates every in-force decision's predicates via getDependencies reachability; a violated predicate means the code drifted from the recorded intent EVEN WITH NO DIFF IN SCOPE (e.g. `pay` stopped reaching `verifySession`). Surfaced as `hunch conform [--strict]` and the hunch_conformance() MCP tool (advertised in grounding). No model in the check — pure graph reachability, preserving the model-out-of-the-block-path moat. Proven by tests: direct-vs-transitive, existence, and drift detection (removing the call flips satisfied→violated).
consequences: Introduces a new, deterministic correctness class — intent-conformance — that catches SILENT intent erosion a diff-only guard structurally cannot see (no file in scope changed, but the intent is now false of the code).
Reuses the existing call/dep graph (getDependencies) — no new analysis engine; the inversion rides infra already shipped (#1 graph).

Honest limits: reachability conflates calls/imports (edge-type precision deferred); predicates are hand-authored today (auto-suggesting them from the birthing commit's analyzeDiff is the natural next step); deletion of the subject is itself reported as a violation.

Strategic seed: if code can be PROVEN against intent, the next steps are generating code that satisfies intent and treating Hunch as the source of truth with code as a cache — the boundary-less direction.
alternatives_rejected: LLM judge of 'does the code satisfy this prose intent' — REJECTED: non-deterministic and off-moat; compile the intent into a graph predicate the dev vouches for instead (same shape as veto tripwires, but for POSITIVE must-hold invariants).
Stay diff-scoped (status quo guards only) — INSUFFICIENT: misses drift where the guarded file wasn't touched but the intent eroded elsewhere in the graph.
Edge-type-precise reachability (calls-only vs imports-only) in v1 — DEFERRED: the unified getDependencies walk is the cheap 90%; refine when a real case needs the distinction.
